content:
# Session 13 (iter-013) — Review Summary

## Metadata
- Model: opus. Two prover lanes: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (D3′), `SliceTransport.lean` (DUAL).
- Sorry count: **before 4** (TOS 2: L712,L3184 + Slice 2: L883,L891) → **after 4** but build state changed:
  - TOS: **GREEN**, 2 sorries (L712 deferred, L3182 target). Net-zero sorries, real prefix/diagnostic progress.
  - Slice: **RED** (regression green→red). 1 sorry (L912 right_inv) + 1 **compile error** (L890) + debug `trace_state` (L911).
- Global build NOT clean: `lake build …SliceTransport` EXIT 1.

## Target 1 — `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` (TOS L3182; D3′ four-square)
Objective: re-canonicalize the iter-006 prefix (kill `reassoc_of% h1` poison) so step (i), the
`D ≫ E = 𝟙` cancellation, fires.
- **WIN (committed, GREEN):** `erw [h1]` splices `S1 → R0≫R1≫R5≫D` **without** `reassoc_of%`; iter-006
  poison removed; `erw [hδ]` applies Sq2b δ-decomposition.
- **BLOCKED — root-caused (definitive):** `h1` (`sheafificationCompPullback_comp`) states its RHS via
  `Scheme.Modules.pullback`/`pullbackComp`, landing in `Z.Modules`. `Scheme.Modules` is a plain
  `def := SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` with its **own** `Category` instance
  `Scheme.Modules.instCategory` (`Hom := Modules.Hom`), distinct from `SheafOfModules`'s. So `R0 ≫ R1`
  composes in `Scheme.Modules.instCategory` while `E = (sheafification …).map (pbComp.inv.app P)`
  composes in `SheafOfModules`'s — defeq-but-not-syntactic `CategoryStruct.comp`. Empirically verified
  this iter (do NOT re-try):
  - `rw [Category.assoc]` on `(R0≫R1≫R5≫D)≫E`: "did not find `(?f ≫ ?g) ≫ ?h`".
  - `simp only [Category.assoc]`: "made no progress".
  - `erw [Category.assoc]`: crosses by defeq but **whnf-timeout 3.2M heartbeats**.
  - `dsimp only [Scheme.Modules, …instCategory]`: goal unchanged.
  - `rw [h1]`/`rw [hδ]` themselves require `erw` (Functor.map/object defeq) — friction is pervasive.
- This is NOT a tactic-search gap; Brick 1 (`sheafifyMap_pullbackComp_hom_inv_id`) is proven. The blocker
  is the wrong category instance in `h1`'s statement. See task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.lean.md.

## Target 2 — `sliceDualTransport` left_inv/right_inv (Slice; DUAL)
Objective (was CONVERGING, "one mechanical alignment from closing"): close left_inv (L883), then mirror
to right_inv (L891).
- Prover filled left_inv with a substantive ~20-line collapse (M-side relabel via `rfl`+`eqToHom_op`,
  strip `restrictScalars`, `erw [hφ]`, then collapse two `X.presheaf` `eqToHom` relabels to `𝟙` via
  `eqToHom_trans`/`map_id`/`ConcreteCategory.congr_hom`).
- **FAILS to compile at L890:** `rw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars.map_apply]` — "Did not find an occurrence
  of the pattern". The wrapper isn't in the spelling `rw` expects (defeq-coerced); needs `erw`/`simp only`
  or goal inspection. Everything around it is plausible; this is one rw-miss away from closing.
- right_inv (L912) untouched; prover left a debug `trace_state` at L911 (committed artifact).
- **Consequence:** the file went **green→red**. The lane ended via a `Monitor` mis-call (turn ended)
  WITHOUT a clean final build, so the broken state was never caught by the prover.

## Why the build is red / `\leanok` removals
`sync_leanok` (iter=13, sha 73d455e) **removed 25** markers (added 0) on `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`.
This is the deterministic, correct consequence of `SliceTransport.lean` failing to build — every decl in
that module (and dependents) loses `\leanok`. **Not laundering.** Markers return once the file is restored
green. No manual `\leanok` override applied.

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- None. No `\mathlibok` to add (no new Mathlib-backed leaf declarations this iter); no `\lean{...}` renames
  (no new top-level decls — TOS `hcancel`/`hmain` were `have`s, removed; Slice edits all inside
  `sliceDualTransport`); no stale `\notready`. The 25 `\leanok` removals are sync's, left as-is.

## Blueprint doctor
Clean — no orphan chapters, no broken `\ref`/`\uses`, no new axioms.

## Coverage debt
`archon dag-query unmatched` = **97** `lean_aux` nodes (unchanged; no new top-level decls this iter). Bulk
stays deferred to the scheduled `Coverage + file-split` phase per the standing multi-iter plan decision;
per-iter delta was already cleared by the plan agent (2 helper entries). Not enumerated here — see the
query output; planner owns the scheduled phase.

## Subagent skips
- lean-auditor: both lanes were pure proof-filling with **no new definitions/refactors landed** (TOS reverted
  its scaffolding `have`s to a documented `sorry`; Slice edits are all inside `sliceDualTransport`). The two
  dominant issues (Slice RED regression, D3′ instance-wall) are already precisely root-caused by the prover
  task_result + progress-critic conv013. Re-auditing yields no new signal.
- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: same — no new decls/signatures to verify against the chapter; `SliceTransport.lean`
  does not currently compile, so a file-vs-chapter check would only restate "doesn't build". The consolidated
  chapter `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` was last gate-cleared and unchanged in load-bearing content.

## Recommendations
See `recommendations.md`. Headline: (1) RESTORE Slice GREEN — fix L890 (`erw`/`simp only`) or revert
L884–903 to `sorry`; remove `trace_state` L911. (2) D3′: dispatch `refactor` subagent — trigger MET.
